events.title: The Art of Street Photography + Photo Walk
events.description:
Capturing Life’s Rhythms – Learn about street photography and go on a mini photo walk around Industry City!
THIS IS AN IN-PERSON EVENT. PLEASE ALSO BRING YOUR CAMERA
Step into the heart of the city and learn the art of street photography! In this immersive class, you’ll explore how to capture authentic moments of everyday life, from candid expressions to the flow of urban landscapes. Discover techniques to find beauty in the ordinary, and develop your eye for composition, timing, and storytelling. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced photographer, this class will sharpen your instincts and help you create powerful images that resonate. This class will inspire you to see the city in a new light, capturing the rhythms and stories that unfold around you every day!
Midway through the workshop, you will be going outside to take some of your own photos and we will reconvene after to discuss what you shot.
Key Takeaways:
– Learn to anticipate and capture candid moments with perfect timing.
– Master composition techniques to create compelling visual stories.
– Understand how to use natural light and shadows in dynamic street scenes.
– Build confidence in photographing people and busy environments.
– Develop a unique photographic style that reflects your perspective on urban life.
events.vendor: The Art of Street Photography + Photo Walk

events.title: A Tiny Desk at The Center.
events.description:
The Tiny Desk Concert series is a fantastic and intimate live music experience originally created by NPR. It features artists performing short sets in a cozy, desk-like setting, often accompanied by a small audience. The concept emphasizes the personal connection between performers and listeners and showcases a variety of genres and talent.
At the center, we plan to host our own version of the Tiny Desk Concerts to highlight local musicians and foster a sense of community. We’ll set up a welcoming and relaxed environment where artists can share their music, stories, and creativity in an intimate setting. The concerts will be open to the public, and we aim to create a space that encourages engagement and connection among attendees.
events.vendor: A Tiny Desk at The Center.

events.title: Ruthlessly Healthy Workplaces: Beyond Perks, Towards Performance
events.description:
What if individual wellbeing wasn’t just a “perk” of your job, but the primary driver of your organization’s strategic success?
Join Nayth Woodrow for a provocative, TED-style presentation designed to challenge everything you think you know about purpose, wellbeing, and workplace culture. In this high-energy session, Nayth moves past traditional HR concepts to dive into the science of the brain and human emotions to reveal what actually makes progress possible in a modern business.
A ruthlessly healthy workplace prioritizes genuine human connection to drive tangible outcomes. Whether you are a founder, an executive leader, or a frontline “upholder” of your team’s values, this session will provide you with the cognitive tools to evaluate your current practices and nudge your organization toward a state of effective growth.
events.vendor: Ruthlessly Healthy Workplaces: Beyond Perks, Towards Performance

events.title: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
events.description:
Join us for a tour of our state-of-the-art sake brewery in Industry City, Brooklyn. See first hand how we turn rice and water into delicious Brooklyn-made premium craft sake. Led by our expert brewery staff, this experience will walk you through our production facility and allow you to take in the sights, sounds and delicious smells of New York’s premier sake brewery. The tour concludes in our taproom where we will lead you a guided tasting flight of 3 kinds of Brooklyn Kura and introduce you to the nuance and variety of our award-winning sake.
Location:
Please arrive promptly in our taproom to start your tour. The location is:
34 34th Street, Inner courtyard
Industry City Building 6
Brooklyn, NY 11232
events.vendor: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting

events.title: Grateful for Biggie – Grateful Dead Meets Notorious B.I.G. with Special Guest Peak
events.description:
Tickets Here
Industry City and Get It Right Music in association with Murmrr Presents are thrilled to present Grateful for Biggie – a night under the stars featuring the ultimate mashup tribute that seamlessly blends the Grateful Dead’s signature grooves with the lyrical genius of Notorious B.I.G. Get ready for a sonic journey that defies genres and sparks new musical dimensions.
This show features special guest opening act Peak!
Event Info
Date: May 8, 2026
Doors: 6:45pm
Peak Opener: 7:00pm
Set 1: 8:15pm
Set 2: 9:30pm
Tickets: $20 per person plus fees in advance/$25 per person day of show
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can get affordable drinks from the venue’s bar and order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall. Concert is rain or shine.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N or R train to 36th street.
More About Grateful for Biggie
Step into a world where time-honored melodies collide with urban poetry, where the rhythms of yesterday’s legends unite with the beats of a modern era. Introducing “Grateful for Biggie,” the ultimate mashup tribute that seamlessly blends the Grateful Dead’s signature grooves with the lyrical genius of Notorious B.I.G. Get ready for a sonic journey that defies genres and sparks new musical dimensions.
At the heart of “Grateful for Biggie” lies the powerful fusion of two iconic forces. The Grateful Dead’s ethereal harmonies and improvisational magic interweave with Biggie’s slick rhymes and storytelling prowess, creating a tapestry of sound that resonates with fans of both worlds. Each performance is a dynamic celebration of musical innovation, where “China Cat Sunflower” meets “Kick in the Door,” and “Shakedown Street” flows seamlessly with “Hypnotize.”
Led by a group of passionate musicians, “Grateful for Biggie” captures the essence of both the Grateful Dead and Notorious B.I.G., paying homage to the legacies that shaped generations. With electrifying stage presence and the soulful vibes of the past, this tribute band invites you to revel in the familiar while experiencing the thrill of the unexpected.
So, whether you’re a Dead Head or a Hip Hop head looking to groove to some legendary tunes, “Grateful for Biggie” promises an unforgettable ride. Join us as we bridge the gap between two of America’s greatest cultural movements of all time, creating a musical experience that proves true artistry knows no boundaries.
More About Peak
In late 2016 Brooklyn-based guitarist and songwriter Jeremy Hilliard began performing and touring under the name PEAK. In 2018, he released the now critically acclaimed debut album Electric Bouquet, produced by Dave Brandwein (Turkuaz). In 2019 the band took on a life of its own as one by one some of the best musicians in the improv rock scene joined the group including Kito Bovenschulte (Particle) on drums, Josh T. Carter (Hayley Jane and The Primates) on bass, and newcomer extraordinaire Johnny Young on keys. The group’s music, described as Psychedelic Indie Funk, is a mix of poetic, melodic songwriting, moody electrofunk, riffed-out blues-rock, and exploratory danceable improvisations. Some of their many influences include artists such as The Band, The Talking Heads, Phish, The Grateful Dead, Radiohead, James Brown, and Miles Davis, as well as modern songwriters such as Conor Oberst, Mac Demarco, Kurt Vile, and Jack White to name a few.
events.vendor: Grateful for Biggie – Grateful Dead Meets Notorious B.I.G. with Special Guest Peak

events.title: Unicorn Boy and the Slumber Party of Doom!
events.description:
In the second exciting installment of this magical adventure, our hero Unicorn Boy sets out in search of deep mystical knowledge: how to fit in at a sleep-over, how to handle everyone seeming cooler than you are, and what’s up with this whole unicorn thing? Perfect for readers of Narwhal and Jelly, Grumpy Unicorn, and InvestiGators.
Brian Reyes always felt a bit different—even before growing a unicorn horn, discovering a talking muffin, and swallowing the ruler of the Underworld to save his BFF. So when he receives a mysterious invitation for a Unicorn-Only Sleepover, Brian wonders if these fellow magical creatures might help him make sense of his increasingly odd world. Held in an enchanted castle in the clouds, not everything at this glitzy, glamorous party is as it seems, and Brian will have to fend off increasing social anxiety, ignore the negative feelings in his gut, and stay true to himself if he’s going to survive the night!
With a wave of his magic pen, Dave Roman has created a cast of charming oddballs reckoning with normal, every day problems—you know, things like heroic destinies and the fate of all magic in the universe. Join Dave and your friends at Powerhouse for a magical book launch event!
events.vendor: Unicorn Boy and the Slumber Party of Doom!

events.title: American Craft Sake Tasting Class and Brewery Tour
events.description:
American-made sake is on the rise—and there’s never been a better time to enjoy it. If you’re curious about sake and eager to dive into the expanding world of U.S.-crafted premium sakes, this class is the perfect place to start your journey. Join us in person at the Sake Studies Center at Brooklyn Kura in NYC to learn the fundamentals of sake, with a special focus on the innovation and craftsmanship emerging from sake breweries across the United States.
In this engaging and beginner-friendly session, you’ll enjoy a guided tasting of five standout American sakes—including pours from Kato Sake Works (NY), Proper Sake Co. (TN), Origami Sake (AR), Den Sake Brewery (CA), and Brooklyn Kura (NY). Along the way, we’ll explore each brewery’s story, brewing philosophy, and how local ingredients and regional culture shape their sake.
Whether you’re brand new to sake or looking to expand your palate, this class offers a fun and flavorful introduction to the emerging world of American sake.
What’s Included:
A welcome sake to kick things off
A “Sake 101” presentation with a focus on the U.S. craft sake movement and regional differences
Guided tasting of 5 premium American-made sakes
Printed educational materials to take home
Info on where to purchase each sake featured
Guided tour of Brooklyn Kura’s brewery
Small bites thoughtfully paired with each sake
So, join us and come taste what’s brewing in the U.S. — you’ll leave with a new appreciation for sake and the American brewers redefining it.
events.vendor: American Craft Sake Tasting Class and Brewery Tour

events.title: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour
events.description:
Set against the iconic backdrop of Sunset Park’s historic waterfront, the massive, revitalized structures of Industry City form a breathtaking stage for an exclusive culinary journey. This premium food and cocktail tour is a curated exploration of taste and craft, transforming an evening into a memorable, high-end experience.
Your adventure begins with artistic bonbons at Lilac Chocolates, explores gourmet imports at Sahadi’s, and includes a refined whiskey tasting at Fort Hamilton Distillery, followed by a sophisticated sake flight. Savor additional artisanal discoveries within the awe-inspiring industrial halls.
Perfect as a distinctive date night, this tour is also fully customizable for private groups and corporate retreats. We specialize in tailoring this engaging experience for team-building and client entertainment, making it an ideal one-day corporate outing. A sophisticated mini-vacation for locals and a profound discovery for visitors.
events.vendor: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour

events.title: A Brooklyn Dream Immersive Play
events.description:
A Brooklyn Dream is a groundbreaking immersive theater experience set inside The Brooklyn Pop Exhibit. Told through the eyes of three generations of a Bay Ridge family, the production blends theater, film, music and installation art into a raw, emotional journey about survival, ambition and the pursuit of dreams.
Audiences step directly into cinematic recreations of Brooklyn streets, stoops, train yards and living rooms – each space alive with actors, archival footage, graffiti and soundscapes that pull you into the past and present. At its heart is the story of Kaves, a graffiti writer and musician navigating 1980s Brooklyn, while uncovering the triumphs and traumas of his grandparents and parents before him. The narrative moves fluidly across decades, juxtaposing the underdog struggles of post-war immigrants with the fire of hip-hop’s golden era.
A Brooklyn Dream is as personal as it is universal. It’s an honest portrait of family, loss, resilience and the power of art to transform pain into beauty. Each performance is both theater and memory, with audiences moving through immersive sets, standing inches from the actors and feeling the heartbeat of Brooklyn itself.
Raw, authentic and based on true events, it is more than a play, it is an experience that blurs the line between art and life.
Tickets available for 5 time slots: 4:30, 5:40, 6:45, 8:00, 9:10
events.vendor: A Brooklyn Dream Immersive Play

events.title: Archetypal Collage: Taurus Season
events.description:
All signs are welcome! This space is for anyone curious about creativity, symbolism, and self-exploration, no matter your chart.
Archetypal Collage: Taurus Season is a creative, reflective workshop blending art-making, astrology, and archetypal symbolism. Part collage, part inner alchemy, this experience invites you to explore your personal mythology through the archetype of Taurus. Connect to your roots through symbolic teachings, collage art, curated playlists, and personalized 1:1 archetypal mini-readings.
Everyone can work with the universal themes of The Bull – groundedness, sensuality, stability, self-worth, endurance, and embodiment. Similar to a tarot reading, you’ll also learn to to “read” your collage creations to understand your unique relationship to these energies.
No prior art experience or astrology knowledge is required. All materials are provided (including vintage magazines!).
events.vendor: Archetypal Collage: Taurus Season

events.title: US Air Guitar Championships – Brooklyn
events.description:
US Air Guitar Championships – Brooklyn Qualifier – The winner goes straight to Nationals in San Francisco!
🎸🔥 Brooklyn Regional: US Air Guitar Championships 2026 🔥🎸
📅 Date: Saturday, May 9th, 2026
🕖 Time: 7 PM – 10 PM
📍 Venue: Barrow’s Intense Tasting Room
📌 Location: 86 34th St, Brooklyn, NY
Brooklyn’s finest air shredders, it’s time to take the stage! The 2026 Brooklyn Regional Competition at Barrow’s Intense is your chance to claim air guitar glory and secure a direct spot in the National Finals in San Francisco, CA on Saturday, August 1st — no extra battles, no second chances, just straight to the big stage!
🎶 What to Expect:
🔥 High-energy performances from air guitarists ready to melt faces (without touching a single string).
🏆 A chance to win the Brooklyn crown and advance straight to the National Competition.
🍹 Incredible drinks at Barrow’s Intense—featuring NYC’s largest selection of locally distilled spirits, plus beer, wine, and non-alcoholic options.
🎤 Hilarious commentary and expert judging to help crown the next air guitar champion.
Why You Should Come:
🤘 To witness Brooklyn’s boldest, wildest, and most entertaining competitors battle for air guitar supremacy.
🎟️ To cheer, laugh, and lose yourself in the ridiculous joy of it all.
🏅 To compete yourself! Sign up now and take your shot at the national stage: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/competitor-sign-up-us-air-guitar-championships-brooklyn-heck-yeah-tickets-1986278894643
If you’ve got the moves, the energy, and the pure rock ‘n’ roll spirit, this is your moment. Reserve your spot to compete or grab a ticket to watch — it’s going to be legendary! 🎸🔥
See you there—and may the best air guitarist win! 🤘💥

events.title: Introduction to Shochu – Tasting Class and Brewery Tour
events.description:
Are you curious about Shochu? Join us to learn the basics of this delicious and unique 500+ year old Japanese Spirit, how to differentiate between styles, and how to serve Shochu to its best advantage and use it in delicious craft cocktails. We’ll also explore the nuance of Shochu vs Sake and Soju! The course includes a guided tour of Brooklyn Kura’s sake brewery (through the lens of shochu making), a flight of 5 different shochus served in a variety of styles, and a few snacks to accompany the tasting.
What’s included:
-Shochu 101 presentation to walk you through the fundamentals of the Japanese spirit
-A guided tasting of 4 different Japanese Shochus, as well as Brooklyn Kura’s house-made Shochu
-A Handout/notes section for reference at home
-Snacks to accompany the spirit tasting
-A 10% discount for purchasing bottles at Brooklyn Kura or select bottles at Kuraichi
events.vendor: Introduction to Shochu – Tasting Class and Brewery Tour
